questions.text: When learning a new concept in class, you prefer:
opts: Diagrams, charts, or visual presentations on the board
Teacher explaining it verbally in detail
Reading from textbooks or written notes
Doing hands-on activities or experiments

questions.text: If you need to assemble something (like a project model), you would:
opts: Look at the picture or diagram provided
Ask someone to explain it step-by-step
Read the instructions carefully first
Start building and figure it out as you go

questions.text: When you get lost in a new place, you typically:
opts: Look at a map or GPS
Ask someone for directions verbally
Read street signs and written directions
Wander around and explore until you find it

questions.text: When remembering important information (like exam dates), you usually:
opts: Visualize it in your mind or write it in color-coded notes
Repeat it aloud several times
Write it down in a planner or notebook
Practice the information repeatedly

questions.text: During a subject like Physics or Chemistry, you learn best through:
opts: Diagrams, molecular models, or flowcharts
Teacher's explanations and class discussions
Reading chapters and working through examples
Lab experiments and practical demonstrations

questions.text: When working on a group project, your role is usually to:
opts: Create visuals, posters, or PowerPoint slides
Lead discussions and present findings to others
Write the content and organize the presentation
Build models or create physical demonstrations

questions.text: For a History or Social Studies topic, you prefer to learn by:
opts: Watching documentaries or viewing maps/timelines
Listening to stories and discussions about events
Reading historical accounts and taking detailed notes
Visiting museums or re-enacting historical events

questions.text: When you struggle with a concept, you tend to:
opts: Draw pictures or create mind maps to understand it
Ask your teacher or friends to explain it verbally
Look for additional reading materials or examples
Practice with real examples or work through problems
Good lighting, organized desk, color-coded materials
Quiet place, or studying with a study buddy to discuss
Books, notes, and written resources all around
Space to move around, fidget tools, or take breaks

questions.text: When learning a new language (English, Hindi, etc.), you do best with:
opts: Written examples and reading comprehension
Listening to native speakers and speaking practice
Grammar rules and written exercises
Role-playing conversations and practical usage

questions.text: During online classes, you find it easier to focus when:
opts: There are slides with visuals and clear structure
The teacher speaks clearly and there's interactive discussion
Detailed notes or transcripts are provided
There are activities, quizzes, or things to do

questions.text: After a lesson, you best retain information by:
opts: Creating summaries with diagrams and colors
Discussing it with classmates or explaining to others
Re-reading and rewriting notes
Applying it through problems, projects, or practice
